q_tail.question: Does the hindwing have a short tail (a narrow finger-like projection) at the end of vein 4?
q_tail.hint: Look at the hindwing outer margin between the tornus (inner corner) and the apex. Vein 4 ends roughly in the middle of this margin. Only two Malaysian Allotinus — A. borneensis and A. fabius — have a projecting tail here; every other species has a smooth, rounded, or finely toothed margin with no tail.

choices.label: Yes — hindwing is tailed
No — hindwing is tailless

q_tailed_apex.question: On the underside, which of the following features do you observe?
q_tailed_apex.hint: Both species have a tailed hindwing and a strongly swollen vein 4. Look for two mutually exclusive characters: A. borneensis has the forewing apex (wing tip) shaded reddish-brown with warmer, redder striations overall. A. fabius has enlarged and blotchy postdiscal spots above vein 4 on the forewing and above vein 6 on the hindwing, with white-edged submarginal spots.

choices.label: Forewing apex shaded reddish-brown, standing out from the rest of the wing
Postdiscal spots enlarged and blotchy above FW vein 4 and HW vein 6
Cannot determine — neither feature clearly visible

r_borneensis.name: Allotinus borneensis
r_borneensis.note: Tailed hindwing (short tail projecting at vein 4), with vein 4 strongly swollen and bearing a visual brand on both wing surfaces. Underside buff-coloured with redder striations than A. fabius, and the forewing apex is shaded reddish-brown. Found in lowland and montane forest of the Malay Peninsula.

r_fabius.name: Allotinus fabius arrius
r_fabius.note: Tailed hindwing (short tail projecting at vein 4 in males), with vein 4 strongly swollen. Underside greyish-white with dense striations; the postdiscal spots above forewing space 4 and hindwing space 6 are enlarged and blotchy, and the black submarginal spots are edged with white on their outer side.

g_tailed_pair.description: Both species are the only Malaysian Allotinus with a tailed hindwing. If the forewing-apex shading can't be checked, compare underside ground colour and the size/blotchiness of the postdiscal spots.
key_features: A. borneensis: redder striations overall, reddish-brown shaded forewing apex.
A. fabius: greyish-white underside, enlarged blotchy postdiscal spots above FW space 4 / HW space 6, white-edged black submarginal spots.
g_tailed_pair.no_next_note: Try Feature Scoring with any other visible underside features to narrow between these two.

q_brand_presence.question: On the underside, is there a visual brand — a patch of specialised pale or dark scales — on the swollen base of hindwing vein 4?
q_brand_presence.hint: Look for a noticeably swollen base of vein 4 on the hindwing, often with a patch of differently-coloured or differently-textured scales (the 'visual brand', a male secondary sexual character).

choices.label: Yes — vein 4 base is swollen with a visible brand
No visible brand, vein 4 base not noticeably swollen

q_brand_hw_margin.question: Is the hindwing outer margin smooth/evenly rounded, or does it have small tooth-like points (dentate)?
q_brand_hw_margin.hint: Examine the outer edge of the hindwing between the tornus and apex for small angular projections at the vein-ends. Dentation can be subtle and is often stronger in females.

choices.label: Smooth or evenly rounded margin
Margin has small tooth-like points (dentate)

q_smooth_margin_6.question: Does the forewing have an extensive white or whitish area, especially toward the wing base (visible on either wing surface)?
q_smooth_margin_6.hint: A. parapus's forewing is white from close to the base, with only the costa and outer margin dark brown. The other smooth-margined, brand-bearing species (A. apries, A. bidiensis, A. portunus, A. sarrastes, A. strigatus) all have a plain or uniformly dark brown forewing with no white area.

choices.label: Yes — forewing has an extensive white/whitish area toward the base
No — forewing is plain or only narrowly marked

r_parapus.name: Allotinus parapus
r_parapus.note: Tailless hindwing, subgenus Paragerydus. Forewing upperside white to the wing base with a dark brown costa and distal margin; hindwing uniformly brown to brownish-grey. Vein 4 base is swollen and bears a visual brand of specialised scales. Underside whitish to pale buff with dense dark dots/striae in an obscure pattern. Recorded from the Gunung Kinabalu area, Borneo.

q_smooth_margin_5.question: On the underside, is the visual brand on hindwing vein 4 a broad oval patch, or a narrow patch running along the vein?
q_smooth_margin_5.hint: Compare the shape of the pale/dark scale patch on the swollen base of vein 4.

choices.label: Broad oval patch
Narrow patch running along vein 4

r_apries.name: Allotinus apries apries
r_apries.note: Tailless hindwing with smooth (non-dentate) margins, subgenus Paragerydus — unlike the dentate-margined A. unicolor and A. leogoron. Male upperside dark brown with a broad oval pale visual brand covering more than half of vein 4, the base of which is swollen; female dark brown with a pale area beyond the end of the cell. Underside pattern poorly documented; forewing postdiscal spots run roughly parallel to the outer margin. Larger than A. corbeti (which has no visual brand).

q_fabitaras_quartet.question: On the hindwing underside postdiscal spot row, is the spot in space 6 displaced toward space 7 — sitting roughly midway between spots 5 and 7 and breaking the row's smooth curve?
q_fabitaras_quartet.hint: The postdiscal row runs between the middle of the wing and the outer margin. In most species spots 2-6 follow a smooth curve; in A. strigatus, spot 6 is offset toward spot 7.

choices.label: Yes — spot 6 is offset toward spot 7, breaking the curve
No — spot 6 follows the same smooth curve as spots 2-5

r_strigatus.name: Allotinus strigatus malayanus
r_strigatus.note: Tailless hindwing with a rounded margin, subgenus Fabitaras. Plain dark brown upperside with vein 4 swollen along about half its length and a visual brand around 2mm wide. Underside pale buff; the hindwing postdiscal spot in space 6 sits roughly midway between spots 5 and 7, breaking the row's smooth curve (unique among its relatives), and the black submarginal spots are white-edged on both wings. A common forest species at low to moderate elevations.

q_postdiscal_dislocation.question: On the hindwing underside, does the postdiscal spot row appear interrupted or offset (dislocated) at vein 4, rather than running in one smooth curve?
q_postdiscal_dislocation.hint: Follow the postdiscal spot row across vein 4 (between hindwing spaces 3/4) and check whether the spots on either side line up smoothly or are offset toward the margin/base relative to each other.

choices.label: Yes — row is dislocated/offset at vein 4
No — row runs in one smooth curve, parallel to the margin

r_sarrastes.name: Allotinus sarrastes
r_sarrastes.note: Tailless hindwing with a rounded margin, subgenus Fabitaras. Plain dark brown upperside with vein 4 strongly swollen along more than half its length. Underside pale buff; the postdiscal spot row is dislocated at vein 4, running closer to the margin at the dorsum than at the costa, and the black submarginal spots are white-edged. One of the more common Fabitaras species, found at low to moderate elevations in the Malay Peninsula.

q_bidiensis_portunus.question: On the hindwing underside, is the visual brand on the swollen base of vein 4 large and obvious, covering more than about half the vein's length?
q_bidiensis_portunus.hint: A. portunus has a strongly developed brand on both wing surfaces. A. bidiensis has a much smaller, inconspicuous brand (under ~1mm).

choices.label: Yes — large, obvious brand covering most of vein 4
No — brand small and inconspicuous (under ~1mm)
Cannot determine — feature not visible

r_portunus.name: Allotinus portunus maitus
r_portunus.note: Tailless hindwing with a rounded/regular margin, subgenus Fabitaras. Male upperside brown with vein 4 strongly swollen for more than half its length and a visual brand on both wing surfaces; female plain brown. Underside whitish to pale buff; the postdiscal spot series runs parallel to the outer margin, with spot 6 in an even arc with spots 2-5 and the outer part of spot 7 well inside spot 6. One of the more frequently encountered Fabitaras species, typically at 600m and above in the Malay Peninsula.

r_bidiensis.name: Allotinus bidiensis
r_bidiensis.note: Tailless hindwing with a rounded margin, subgenus Fabitaras. Plain dark brown upperside on both wings, with only a small and inconspicuous visual brand (under 1mm) on vein 4. Underside whitish with dense dark scales and striae; the hindwing postdiscal stria in space 6 sits well toward the margin relative to space 7. A Bornean species recorded from Gunung Kinabalu, Keningau, and Sarawak.

g_bidiensis_portunus.description: Both have a smoothly-curved hindwing postdiscal spot row and a pale underside. They differ mainly in how prominent the visual brand on vein 4 is.
key_features: A. portunus: large, obvious brand covering more than half of vein 4 (Malay Peninsula, ≥600m).
A. bidiensis: small, inconspicuous brand under ~1mm (Borneo).
g_bidiensis_portunus.no_next_note: Locality is a useful cross-check: A. bidiensis is recorded only from Borneo, A. portunus from the Malay Peninsula.

q_dentate_group.question: What is the underside ground colour?
q_dentate_group.hint: Compare the overall background tone of the underside — warm buffy-brown versus cooler whitish/grey.

choices.label: Pale buff / warm buffy-brown
Whitish or greyish-white

q_horsfieldi_melos.question: On the hindwing underside, does the visual brand on vein 4 form a narrow patch that touches vein 3 only briefly near its base, or a broader patch covering both sides of the swollen vein-4 base?
q_horsfieldi_melos.hint: A. horsfieldi has a narrow brand reaching vein 3 only at its base; A. melos (very locally known, from Kinabalu Park) has a broader brand on both sides of vein 4's base.

choices.label: Narrow brand, touching vein 3 only near its base
Broader brand covering both sides of vein-4 base
Cannot determine — feature not visible

r_horsfieldi.name: Allotinus horsfieldi permagnus
r_horsfieldi.note: Tailless hindwing, weakly dentate in males and more strongly dentate in females, subgenus Paragerydus. Forewing somewhat produced; male upperside reddish-brown with a narrow visual brand extending more than half of vein 4 and touching vein 3 only near its base. Underside pale buff, more densely striate than its relatives, with the postdiscal fascia dislocated at veins 4 and 2. A common species of lowland jungle, often found alongside A. unicolor (which is smaller).

r_melos.name: Allotinus melos
r_melos.note: Tailless hindwing (subgenus Paragerydus traits: hindwing may be dentate/sinuate, especially in females). Vein 4 base is swollen with a visual brand on both sides. Underside white to pale buff. Very poorly documented — known only from a systematic checklist record at Poring Hot Springs, Kinabalu Park, Sabah; identification should be treated as provisional pending better reference photos.

g_horsfieldi_melos.description: Both are pale-buff-underside Paragerydus with a dentate hindwing and a visual brand on vein 4, but A. melos is known from a single very restricted Bornean locality.
key_features: A. horsfieldi: narrow brand touching vein 3 only near its base; common, lowland jungle, often with A. unicolor.
A. melos: broader brand on both sides of vein-4 base; known only from Poring Hot Springs, Kinabalu Park, Sabah.
g_horsfieldi_melos.no_next_note: A. melos is extremely locally restricted (Kinabalu Park, Sabah) — A. horsfieldi is by far the more likely identification elsewhere.

q_leogoron_unicolor.question: On the hindwing underside, is the visual brand on vein 4 a broad oval patch extending well along vein 3 for most of its length?
q_leogoron_unicolor.hint: A. leogoron has a broad oval brand reaching well along vein 3. A. unicolor's brand is narrower, covering roughly half the length of vein 4 only.

choices.label: Yes — broad oval brand extending well along vein 3
No — brand narrower, covering roughly half of vein 4 only
Cannot determine — feature not visible

r_leogoron.name: Allotinus leogoron leogoron
r_leogoron.note: Tailless hindwing with narrow dentations, subgenus Paragerydus. Brown upperside with a broad oval visual brand on vein 4 extending well along vein 3. Underside clean greyish-white (whiter than A. horsfieldi's buff), with darker and better-defined striations and the postdiscal fascia dislocated at veins 4 and 2. A rare species of lowland forest, closely related to and found in similar habitats to A. horsfieldi.

r_unicolor.name: Allotinus unicolor unicolor
r_unicolor.note: Tailless hindwing (females dentately sinuate), subgenus Paragerydus. Male upperside dark brown with a narrow visual brand astride the swollen vein 4, covering only about half its length; female reddish-brown with a less-pointed forewing. Underside greyish-white (sometimes darker grey) with dense, well-defined dots and striae; hindwing postdiscal spot 6 sits well inside the outer part of spot 7. A common species of lowland jungle, smaller than A. horsfieldi.

g_leogoron_unicolor.description: Both are whitish/greyish-underside, dentate-hindwing Paragerydus with a visual brand on vein 4. They differ mainly in how far the brand extends along vein 3, and are most reliably separated by genitalia (uncus shape) — not visible in photographs.
key_features: A. leogoron: broad oval brand extending well along vein 3; rare; darker, better-defined underside striations.
A. unicolor: narrower brand covering about half of vein 4; common; smaller overall.
g_leogoron_unicolor.no_next_note: A. unicolor is common and A. leogoron rare — in the absence of a clear brand view, A. unicolor is the more likely identification.

q_tornal_patch.question: On the underside, is there a white patch in the tornal area (the inner corner) of the hindwing?
q_tornal_patch.hint: The tornus is the rounded inner corner of the hindwing, closest to the body and abdomen. Only A. brooksi shows a distinct white patch here.

choices.label: Yes — white patch in the hindwing tornal area
No — tornal area is the same colour as the rest of the wing

r_brooksi.name: Allotinus brooksi
r_brooksi.note: Tailless hindwing, subgenus Fabitaras. Plain dark brown upperside on both wings. Underside whitish with a distinct dark central spot mid-wing and a white patch in the hindwing tornal (inner corner) area — its key diagnostic feature. A Bornean endemic recorded from Bau, Sarawak.

q_no_brand_color.question: Is the underside ground colour pale buff (warm buffy-brown), or whitish to greyish-white (cooler tone)?
q_no_brand_color.hint: Compare the overall background tone of the hindwing and forewing underside. A. davidis and A. nivalis have a warm pale-buff ground colour; the remaining species without a visual brand (A. albifasciatus, A. fallax, A. otsukai, A. subviolaceus, A. agnolia, A. corbeti, A. substrigosus) have a cooler whitish or greyish-white ground colour.

choices.label: Pale buff / warm buffy-brown
Whitish or greyish-white

q_davidis_nivalis.question: On the hindwing underside, is the central spot in space 7 (near the costa, mid-wing) noticeably blackened compared to the surrounding spots?
q_davidis_nivalis.hint: Space 7 lies along the hindwing costa. A. davidis shows a blackened central spot here (and a blackened postdiscal spot on its inner edge); A. nivalis does not.

choices.label: Yes — central spot in space 7 is blackened
No — central spot in space 7 is not noticeably darker
Cannot determine — feature not visible

r_davidis.name: Allotinus davidis
r_davidis.note: Tailless hindwing, subgenus Paragerydus. A small species (forewing length 9-14mm) with brown upperside, a small white costal fleck near the forewing apex, and no visual brand in the male. Underside pale buff with dense, well-defined dots and striae; the hindwing central spot in space 7 and the postdiscal spot on its inner edge are both blackened — the key feature distinguishing it from A. nivalis and A. substrigosus.

r_nivalis.name: Allotinus nivalis
r_nivalis.note: Tailless hindwing, subgenus Paragerydus. Brown upperside with a small white costal fleck near the forewing apex, and no visual brand in the male. Underside pale buff with dense dots and striae; unlike A. davidis, the hindwing central spot in space 7 is not blackened. A Bornean species, larger than its close relatives (forewing length 12.5-15.5mm).

g_davidis_nivalis.description: Both have a small white forewing costal fleck, no visual brand, and a pale buff underside with dense spotting. They differ mainly in whether the hindwing central spot in space 7 is blackened.
key_features: A. davidis: hindwing central spot 7 and postdiscal spot 7 (inner edge) both blackened.
A. nivalis: hindwing central spot 7 not noticeably darker; larger on average (FW 12.5-15.5mm).
g_davidis_nivalis.no_next_note: If the hindwing space-7 spots aren't clearly visible, compare overall size and locality (A. nivalis is recorded from Borneo).

q_costal_spot.question: On the hindwing underside, is there a prominent blackish spot below the centre of the costa (leading edge), distinct from the rest of the spot pattern?
q_costal_spot.hint: This spot, if present, sits apart from the regular row of postdiscal/submarginal spots and is the easiest way to recognise A. substrigosus.

choices.label: Yes — prominent blackish spot below the costa centre
No such spot

r_substrigosus.name: Allotinus substrigosus substrigosus
r_substrigosus.note: Tailless hindwing, subgenus Paragerydus. A very small species (forewing length 9-14mm) with brown upperside, a small white costal fleck near the forewing apex, and no visual brand. Underside whitish with dense, obscure dark dots and striae; most easily recognised by a prominent blackish spot below the centre of the hindwing costa, and the hindwing postdiscal spot in space 7 is not blackened (cf. A. davidis). Found in lowland jungle.

q_white_band.question: Does the hindwing show a broad white band or area (visible on either wing surface)?
q_white_band.hint: A. albifasciatus is the only species in this group with a broad white band across the hindwing, in addition to white areas on the forewing. The others (A. fallax, A. otsukai, A. subviolaceus, A. agnolia, A. corbeti) have a plain brown hindwing, at most with isolated pale flecks.

choices.label: Yes — hindwing has a broad white band/area
No — hindwing is plain brown, no white band

r_albifasciatus.name: Allotinus albifasciatus
r_albifasciatus.note: Tailless hindwing with regular margins. The rarest Malaysian Allotinus: forewing upperside has white areas and the hindwing has a broad white band. Vein 4 is not or only weakly swollen and veins 6/7 are separate. Underside greyish-white, densely marked with dark dots and striae — whiter and more blotchy than A. fallax. Recorded from the Malay Peninsula and northern Sumatra, mostly above 750 m.

q_fw_white.question: Does the forewing show an extensive white area, especially toward the wing base (visible on either wing surface)?
q_fw_white.hint: A. fallax and A. otsukai both have a forewing that is extensively white or sullied white from near the base, with only the costa, outer margin (and in A. fallax, a broad border) remaining dark brown. A. subviolaceus, A. agnolia, and A. corbeti lack this — their forewings are plain brown, reddish-brown, or show at most a bluish/violet sheen.

choices.label: Yes — forewing has an extensive white area toward the base
No — forewing is plain or only narrowly marked

q_fallax_otsukai.question: On the forewing upperside, does the white area extend all the way to the wing base (near the body), or is it separated from the base by a brown border?
q_fallax_otsukai.hint: A. otsukai's forewing is sullied white right to the base, with only the costa and outer margin dark brown. A. fallax's white area is framed by a broad brown border and does not reach the base.

choices.label: White reaches the wing base
White area is bordered by brown, does not reach the base
Cannot determine — feature not visible

r_otsukai.name: Allotinus otsukai
r_otsukai.note: Tailless hindwing with regular margins, nominate subgenus Allotinus. Forewing upperside sullied white right to the wing base, with dark brown costal and distal margins; hindwing plain dark brown with no white or blue areas. Underside greyish-white with dense dark dots and striae in an obscure pattern. A Bornean endemic from the Gunung Kinabalu area and Mamut.

r_fallax.name: Allotinus fallax apus
r_fallax.note: Tailless hindwing with smooth margins, nominate subgenus Allotinus. Forewing upperside white with a broad brown border that does not reach the wing base; hindwing plain brown. Vein 4 is not or only slightly swollen and veins 6/7 are separate. Underside white/greyish with dense dark dots and striae — less white and less blotchy than A. albifasciatus. An uncommon species of lowland forest.

g_fallax_otsukai.description: Both have a white-marked forewing upperside with a plain brown hindwing and a similar greyish-white underside. They are best told apart by how far the white extends toward the forewing base.
key_features: A. otsukai: forewing white reaches the wing base (Bornean endemic, Kinabalu area).
A. fallax: forewing white is bordered by brown and does not reach the base (Malay Peninsula lowland forest).
g_fallax_otsukai.no_next_note: Check locality — A. otsukai is a Bornean endemic, while A. fallax occurs on the Malay Peninsula.

q_blue_sheen.question: On the forewing upperside, is there a bluish or violet sheen with a broad dark brown border?
q_blue_sheen.hint: A. subviolaceus is recognised by a greyish-blue sheen across the forewing upperside in both sexes, bordered by a broad dark brown margin. A. agnolia is dark brown with veins 6/7 stalked near the forewing apex; A. corbeti is uniformly reddish-brown with a rounded forewing apex — neither shows a blue or violet sheen.

choices.label: Yes — bluish/violet sheen with a broad dark brown border
No — no blue/violet sheen

r_subviolaceus.name: Allotinus subviolaceus subviolaceus
r_subviolaceus.note: Tailless hindwing with regular (non-dentate) margins. The most frequently encountered nominate-subgenus Allotinus: forewing upperside has a greyish-blue sheen (in both sexes) with a broad dark brown border, vein 4 is not or only weakly swollen, and veins 6 and 7 are separate (not stalked). Underside greyish-white with dense brown streaks and spots in an obscure Lycaenid pattern.

q_corbeti_agnolia.question: Is the upperside uniformly reddish-brown, or dark/blackish brown?
q_corbeti_agnolia.hint: A. corbeti is a very small species (forewing length 10-12mm) with a uniform reddish-brown upperside and rounded forewing apex. A. agnolia is darker, with veins 6 and 7 stalked near the forewing apex (an unusual venation feature) — primarily a Sumatran species occasionally noted in regional keys.

choices.label: Uniformly reddish-brown
Dark or blackish brown
Cannot determine — feature not visible

r_corbeti.name: Allotinus corbeti
r_corbeti.note: Tailless hindwing, subgenus Paragerydus. A very small species (forewing length 10-12mm) with a uniformly reddish-brown upperside in both sexes, a rounded forewing apex, vein 4 swollen only about a third of its length, and no visual brand. Underside pattern resembles a miniature A. leogoron.

r_agnolia.name: Allotinus agnolia
r_agnolia.note: Tailless hindwing, nominate subgenus Allotinus. Dark upperside with vein 4 only weakly swollen; uniquely among Malaysian Allotinus, veins 6 and 7 are stalked near the forewing apex rather than separate. Underside whitish/greyish with obscure dark dots and striae. Primarily a Sumatran species, occasionally noted in regional identification keys.

g_corbeti_agnolia.description: Both lack a hindwing visual brand and a forewing costal fleck, but differ in overall colour and size.
key_features: A. corbeti: very small (FW 10-12mm), uniformly reddish-brown, rounded forewing apex.
A. agnolia: darker brown, veins 6/7 stalked near the forewing apex — primarily Sumatran.
g_corbeti_agnolia.no_next_note: Check forewing size and overall tone, and look closely at vein 6/7 near the forewing apex if a clear dorsal photo is available.
